summary: Verified social link new-tab popup fix on Rajat portfolio. All 4 targeted checks PASS: LinkedIn, GitHub (Contact), Phone href, and Project modal GitHub link. Clicking the social anchors triggers window.open with a popup containing the correct URL while the original portfolio page remains unchanged (URL + scrollY identical before/after). Modal also remains open after clicking modal-github-link.
test_method: Playwright context.expect_event('page') to capture new tab/popup; verified popup URL contains expected substring; verified main page url + scrollY identical before/after click; for modal, used JS click to bypass horizontal-scroll transform constraints, then captured popup and asserted modal remains visible.
critical_code_review_comments: Contact.jsx social anchors still keep href + target=_blank which is correct (graceful fallback) and onClick preventDefault+window.open ensures top-level popup even when site is embedded in an iframe — fix is correct and minimal.
Same correct pattern applied in Projects.jsx for modal-github-link and modal-demo-link.
Minor (non-blocking): three.js warning 'THREE.Clock has been deprecated, use THREE.Timer' appears 4x in console — cosmetic only.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Preloader can be skipped by setting sessionStorage['rajat-seen']='1' before reload. Contact section is at scrollY≈10031 in 1920x1080 viewport. project-card-rag is in a horizontal-scroll strip — to open its modal reliably from any scroll position, call element.click() via page.evaluate rather than playwright .click() to bypass transform/visibility checks. Use context.expect_event('page') for popup capture (NOT current-page url assertion — the fix's purpose is that current page stays put while a new tab opens).
